name: Full Corpus Appendix — Milestone Route
description: Prerequisite route for Full Corpus Appendix, segmented into milestone episodes by the progression-router planner. Each phase closes on a quest/unlock capstone rather than an arbitrary skill number.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Chickens / Waterfall Quest.

hints.note: Bury bones for free Prayer xp.

media.caption: Arrive at Lumbridge East chicken farm (train-attack-10)
Player at the coop; chickens are the kill target

methods.location: Lumbridge: coop north-east of the castle just east of the River Lum, or Farmer Fred's pen south of the river
methods.notes: 3-Hitpoint kills mean tiny xp each, but no food is ever needed, feathers stack for pocket money, and the constant bone drops feed early Prayer.

methods.location: Baxtorian Falls (quest run)
methods.notes: One completion at level 1 hands 13,750 Attack + 13,750 Strength xp and lands you at 30/30 with zero quest requirements - the page's fastest 1-30 route. Aggressive level 84-86 monsters roam the quest area, so a few Defence/Hitpoints levels first makes it safer.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Cows / Rock Crab / Waterfall Quest.

hints.note: Aggro stops after 10-15min; crowded spot.

methods.location: Lumbridge East Farm cow pen; quieter herd by the windmill north-west of Lumbridge
methods.notes: Slow but safe low-defence targets steps from the chicken coop; bones for Prayer, hides only worth keeping if early Crafting is next.

methods.location: North of Rellekka; Waterbirth Island

quests: The Fremennik Trials started for free Waterbirth travel (1,000-coin boat fare otherwise); spots with no requirement exist

methods.notes: 50 Hitpoints against almost no Attack or Defence, so food barely matters; packs lose aggro after 10-15 minutes - walk a screen away and back to re-anger them.

methods.notes: Hitpoints has no loop of its own here: every damage point you land pays 1.33 Hitpoints xp on top of the 4 xp to your selected style, so the early melee arc (chickens, then cows) carries this band automatically. You start at Hitpoints 10 with 1,154 xp banked, so only 3,316 xp separates you from 20. The Lumbridge East Farm chicken and cow spawns are free-to-play.

Members status is per spot, not per method: Rock Crabs are members-only everywhere, while the Varrock Sewers moss giants are free-to-play.

methods.location: Edgeville Dungeon (low Wilderness); Chaos Druid Tower north-west of East Ardougne (Thieving 46); Yanille Agility Dungeon (Agility 57 or Thieving 82)
methods.notes: Level 13 with 20 Hitpoints and a max melee hit of 2; the steady herb drops bankroll early Herblore. Thieving 46 gates only the Tower spot. The Edgeville Dungeon spot is Wilderness - those druids also drop looting bags, and PKers can reach you.

methods.location: Varrock Sewers (free-to-play); Catacombs of Kourend (members)

After Monkey Madness II, maniacal monkeys are stated to be faster still; red/black chinchompas do the Ranged equivalent.

methods.location: anywhere (right-click Bury each bones drop; no altar needed)
methods.notes: 4.5 xp per regular bone buried (page's own bury-XP column). Whole band = 5,624 xp, so ~1,250 bones (ceil 5624/4.5) if burial alone carries it. The page's own Starting off section pushes quest xp instead of plain burial.

methods.location: various (The Restless Ghost, Priest in Peril, Recruitment Drive, Holy Grail, Spirits of the Elid)
methods.notes: Page states the first four quests together grant 14,531 Prayer xp (level 1 to 30), with Spirits of the Elid adding 8,000 more - skips early bone-burying entirely.

methods.method: Bury bones from your own early combat kills (zero extra time)
methods.location: wherever you already fight - Lumbridge chicken coop and cow field
methods.notes: Same 4.5 xp per bone as plain burial, executed as an embed inside the train-attack/strength/defence kill loops (mirrors existing steps.jsonl row embed-bury-bones). No travel, no bank, no cost.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Bury big bones / Offer bones at a gilded altar / Offer bones at the Chaos Temple altar / Grind big bones at the Ectofuntus (400% bury xp).

methods.location: anywhere (right-click Bury; no altar needed)
methods.notes: 15 xp per big bone buried (page bury-XP column; the item's own page agrees). Band = 44,715 xp, so ~2,981 buried bones. Members flag grounded false this pass: Big bones' own infobox says members No (prior pass left ??).

methods.method: Offer bones at a gilded altar
methods.location: player-owned house gilded altar (or a hosted altar - official worlds 330/426/465/512, Rimmington house portal)
methods.notes: Big bones give 52.5 xp each with both burners lit (350%); the page's comparison table states 133,875 xp/hr for big bones at 2,550 bones/hr. Construction 75 is boostable (67 with crystal saw + orange spicy stew; 69 guaranteed with saw + house tea), and a host's altar waives it entirely. ~852 big bones cover the band.

methods.method: Offer bones at the Chaos Temple altar
methods.location: Chaos Temple, level 38 Wilderness, west of the Lava Maze (multi-combat, player-killer risk)
methods.notes: Same 350% as a gilded altar plus a 50% chance per offering to not consume the bone - 105 xp average per big bone, ~426 bones for the band. Comparison table states 105,000 xp/hr for big bones. Members flag grounded true this pass: the hut's infobox says the location is F2P but using bones on the altar is members-only.

methods.method: Grind big bones at the Ectofuntus (400% bury xp)
methods.location: Ectofuntus temple, north of Port Phasmatys
methods.notes: 60 xp per big bone (4x bury). Slow: ~180 worships/hr doing your own grinding and slime runs (9 bones + 9 pots + 9 buckets per trip). Medium+ Morytania Diary lets Robin convert 13/26/39 bones to bonemeal+slime daily. Pays 5 ecto-tokens per worship.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Offer dragon bones at a gilded altar / Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar / Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar / Bless bones into shards and offer them at the Teomat libation bowl.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at a gilded altar
methods.location: player-owned house gilded altar (or a hosted altar - official worlds 330/426/465/512, Rimmington house portal)
methods.notes: Page states 643,000 xp/hr for dragon bones with both incense burners lit (252 xp per bone at 2,550 bones/hr). Burners are lit with a marrentill each (Firemaking 30). A host's altar removes the Construction 75 requirement; Phials at Rimmington un-notes bones for 5 coins each.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar
methods.location: Chaos Temple, level 38 Wilderness (multi-combat, player-killer risk)
methods.notes: Page prose states up to 860,000 xp/hr bringing noted dragon bones (500,000 if suiciding each trip); the comparison table separately states 504,000 xp/hr at 2,000 bones offered/hr - different bones/hr assumptions, both cited. 50% chance per offering to keep the bone = 504 xp average per bone consumed, so ~146 dragon bones cover this band. Members-only altar benefit per the hut's infobox.

methods.method: Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar
methods.location: Arceuus, Dark Altar (fairy ring code CIS)
methods.notes: Safer, slower fallback with no Construction gate, fed by ensouled heads gathered as monster drops. The page's rate table only gives concrete hourly figures for the Magic-90 dragon-head tier; mid-tier heads (Magic 41 reanimations) carry per-head xp only.

methods.method: Bless bones into shards and offer them at the Teomat libation bowl
methods.location: the Teomat (Varlamore) - exposed altar + libation bowl
methods.notes: Bless bones at the exposed altar, chisel them into blessed bone shards (5 xp each, 6 with blessed sunfire wine), pour blessed wine into the bowl (1 jug per 400 shards), sacrifice up to 100 shards per action. The page's xp/hr table is keyed by Prayer-level bracket (40-46: ~820,000-980,000; 48-60: ~923,000-1,100,000) - the band spans brackets, so no single figure.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Offer wyvern/dragon bones at a gilded altar / Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar / Reanimate ensouled dragon heads near the Dark Altar / Bless bones into shards and offer them at the Teomat libation bowl.

methods.method: Offer wyvern/dragon bones at a gilded altar
methods.location: player-owned house gilded altar (or a hosted altar - official worlds 330/426/465/512, Rimmington house portal)
methods.notes: 643,000 xp/hr with dragon bones, both burners lit. Wyvern bones are worth exactly the same 72 bury / 252 altar xp as dragon bones (the altar page's own per-bone table rowspans them together; comparison table gives both 642,600). Wyvern-bone sourcing is not grounded in a fetched page - ??.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar
methods.location: Chaos Temple, level 38 Wilderness (multi-combat, player-killer risk)
methods.notes: Up to 860,000 xp/hr with noted dragon bones un-noted at the Elder Chaos druid (50 coins each); 500,000 xp/hr on the suicide strategy; the comparison table's 504,000 figure assumes 2,000 bones offered/hr - cite both, different assumptions. ~1,930 dragon bones cover the band at 504 xp average per bone consumed.

methods.method: Reanimate ensouled dragon heads near the Dark Altar
methods.location: Arceuus, Dark Altar (fairy ring code CIS)
methods.notes: Page table states 343,200 xp/hr reanimating and killing 220 ensouled dragon heads per hour (Magic 90); heads come as monster drops rather than purchases. Passive Magic and combat xp on the side.

methods.method: Bless bones into shards and offer them at the Teomat libation bowl
methods.location: the Teomat (Varlamore) - exposed altar + libation bowl
methods.notes: Same shard loop as band 52. Bracket rates rise with Prayer level (48-60: ~923,000-1,100,000; 62-86: ~1,050,000-1,124,000) but the band spans brackets, so xp_hr stays ??. Making your own shards from bones roughly doubles total time versus sacrificing pre-made shards (page's own worked dragon-bone example).

methods.location: Ice Giants at White Wolf Mountain or the Asgarnian Ice Dungeon (100% Fire weakness, safespottable, no quest reqs); Ghosts in the NW passage of the Stronghold of Security bottom floor (50% Air weakness)
methods.notes: A 100% weakness doubles the matching element's spell damage (strikes cap 16, bolts 24 - 30 with Chaos gauntlets). From Magic+Attack 30 a smoke battlestaff adds 10% accuracy/damage on the standard book. No flat xp/h stated.

methods.location: Rimmington / Wizards' Tower
methods.notes: The two no-req quests pay 1,200 Magic xp together = level 10 from scratch. Adding Fairytale I or The Grand Tree, then Watchtower, plus RFD's Lumbridge Guide subquest banks 20,900 more xp for level 34 without a single cast.

methods.location: Sand Crabs on the Hosidius south shore (60 hp, no quest); Rock Crabs at Rellekka; Ammonite Crabs on Fossil Island (100 hp, Bone Voyage) are the best if unlocked
methods.notes: Crabs hit for almost nothing, have huge hp pools and near-zero Defence, and stay aggressive for 10 minutes at a time; the page recommends 30+ Defence or some food. Fire Strike is the budget spell; Chaos gauntlets push Fire Bolt to one under Fire Blast's max.

methods.method: Enchant Crossbow Bolt ladder (opal 4 / sapphire 7 / pearl 24)
methods.location: anywhere - stackable bolts + runes, castable while running or questing
methods.notes: 159,500/h is the pearl-tier (Magic 24, 29 xp/cast, 1 cosmic + 2 water per set of 10) MANUAL-casting figure (~5,500 casts/h per the table's own footnote; autocast runs at about a third of that). Opal is 9 xp, sapphire 17 xp. Very expensive per xp; bolts must be self-fletched under gather-not-GE.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Iron ore drop-mining (triangle spots) / Tick-manipulated iron (3-tick) / Skip via quest xp rewards.

media.caption: Iron rocks (Mine) at the Al Kharid mine — train-mining-30

methods.location: Al Kharid mine iron triangle (or any of the page's 16 three-rock triangle sites)
methods.notes: Page cites ~45,000-55,000 xp/hr for the whole below-60 iron range without tick manipulation (one flat range, not per band). Iron rocks are F2P, 35 xp/ore, 5.4s respawn. Varrock armour 1+ adds a 10% chance of a double ore.

methods.location: Legends' Guild mine or Isle of Souls mine (4 iron rocks close together)
methods.notes: Faster than triangle drop-mining but much harder; the page recommends it only for players who plan to 3-tick granite from 45 onward. No xp/hr figure cited for the iron variant.

methods.location: Doric's Quest, The Dig Site, Plague City, The Giant Dwarf, The Lost Tribe, Another Slice of H.A.M.
methods.notes: Same six-quest 27,525-xp package as the previous band; it carries a player through 30 on the way to 37.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Iron ore drop-mining (triangle spots) / Motherlode Mine / Granite 3-tick mining.

methods.location: Al Kharid mine iron triangle (or any of the page's 16 triangle sites)
methods.notes: Continues the prior band unchanged; the ~45,000-55,000 xp/hr figure covers the whole below-60 range, not this band specifically.

methods.location: Motherlode Mine, inside the Dwarven Mine (skills necklace teleport to the Mining Guild gate)
methods.notes: Low-effort, profitable alternative; Mining 30 (no boosts) to mine the veins. Its own per-level table scales continuously (13,000 xp/hr at 30, 26,000 at 40, 33,000 at 50), so no single figure covers this band. Members-only.

methods.location: Bandit Camp Quarry or Cape Conch mine
methods.notes: Unlocks at exactly this band's target level (45), so it cannot be a path TO 45 -- listed because the page names it the fastest xp from 45 onward (87,000 xp/hr at 45 with full setup). Atoms for it start in the next band. Members-only.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Granite 3-tick mining / Motherlode Mine / Iron ore (pay-to-play Mining Guild).

methods.location: Bandit Camp Quarry (needs desert-heat protection) or Cape Conch mine (partial Troubled Tortugans, no heat)
methods.notes: Fastest xp from 45 onward. The page's per-level table (with Varrock armour + prospector kit + celestial ring) climbs continuously: 87,000 xp/hr at 45, 95,000 at 55, 103,000 at 65 -- no single band figure. Without tick manipulation only ~63,000 at best. Members-only.

methods.location: Motherlode Mine, inside the Dwarven Mine (skills necklace teleport)
methods.notes: Low-effort alternative; per-level table gives 33,000 xp/hr at 50 and 45,000 at 61 inside this band. The upper level (Mining 57 + one-time 100 golden nuggets) has 64 closer-packed veins on a slower 36-40s deplete timer. Members-only.

methods.location: Members-only area of the Mining Guild, south-east Falador / south Dwarven Mine
methods.notes: Only opens at this band's target level (60, boostable), so it is a next-band option: halved 2.4s iron respawn removes the need for tick manipulation; Iron_rocks cites up to 70,000 xp/hr, the training page 70,000-80,000 depending on performance/pickaxe. The guild zone itself is F2P at 60 Mining; the halved-respawn iron area is the members-only sub-area.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Motherlode Mine / Granite 3-tick mining / Volcanic Mine / Blast Mine.

methods.location: Motherlode Mine upper level, inside the Dwarven Mine (skills necklace teleport)
methods.notes: The band's continuity pick: low-effort with banked profit and nugget-bought upgrades. Per-level table gives 45,000 xp/hr at 61 and 48,000 at 70 inside this band. Members-only.

methods.location: Bandit Camp Quarry or Cape Conch mine
methods.notes: Still the fastest xp through this band: table shows 103,000 xp/hr at 65 and 109,000 at 75.

methods.location: Volcanic Mine, under the Fossil Island Volcano (NE corner entrance, Petrified Pete)
methods.notes: Motherlode's own comparison section states Volcanic Mine always offers the highest rates behind 3-tick granite. Entry: Mining 50 + 30 numulite per visit (or one-time 3,000 for permanent access). Rewards table at 75: 65,200/69,000/75,000 xp/hr for rune/dragon/crystal pickaxe -- gear-dependent, no single figure. Members-only; the minigame is flagged dangerous (fragments are lost on death/teleport).

methods.location: Blast mine, northern Lovakengj
methods.notes: Playable from Mining 43 but the training page explicitly discourages it before 75 (no runite yet, which is most of the profit) -- treat as a next-band option unlocking at this band's top edge. Members-only.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Granite 3-tick mining / Blast Mine / Volcanic Mine / Motherlode Mine.

methods.location: Bandit Camp Quarry or Cape Conch mine
methods.notes: Fastest Mining xp in the game: 109,000 xp/hr at 75 rising to 126,000 at 99 on the page's table, ~134,000 ceiling at max efficiency, 120,000-125,000 realistic with mistakes. Members-only.

methods.location: Blast mine, northern Lovakengj
methods.notes: Profit-focused alternative, best from 75 (runite as if 10 levels higher). The two sources disagree on rates: the training page says 65,000-76,000 xp/hr at 75 rising to 87,000-101,000 at 99; the Blast mine page itself says ~55,000 at 70 to 88,000 at 99 (330 dynamite/hr) -- both cited, neither picked. Plus flat 16,500 Firemaking xp/hr. Members-only.

methods.location: Volcanic Mine, under the Fossil Island Volcano
methods.notes: Second-fastest behind 3-tick granite per Motherlode's comparison. At 99: 79,300/84,000/94,000 xp/hr for rune/dragon/crystal pickaxe; prose estimate 85,000-95,000 with good teams. Runite fragments (best value) from Mining 85. Members-only.

methods.location: Motherlode Mine upper level
methods.notes: Lowest-effort option: 59,000 xp/hr at 90 and 64,000 at 99 with the fully-upgraded loadout (dragon pickaxe, prospector kit, upper level + Super Hopper). Members-only.

methods.notes: Continuity method; no requirements, cannot fail any obstacle, so no food is needed. Course has dropped marks of grace since the 8 May 2024 rebalance.

methods.location: Draynor Village, rough wall in the wheat field north of the bank

methods.location: various quest start points
methods.notes: Completing The Tourist Trap, Recruitment Drive, The Depths of Despair, and The Grand Tree grants 19,700 lump-sum Agility xp (1->33); Tourist Trap alone reaches ~level 26. All four are members quests.

No single blended rate is stated for using both in sequence, so xp_hr stays unverified rather than averaged.

methods.location: Brimhaven Agility Arena (beneath Brimhaven)
methods.notes: Page's own fastest-xp method for this range; every obstacle passable from level 40, rate applies when tagging every pillar and clearing the floor-spikes trap during downtime.

methods.location: various quest start points
methods.notes: Only covers the lower portion of this band (up to level 33); listed since it overlaps the band's start. All four quests are members quests.

methods.location: Various quest lines
methods.notes: Flat 7,200 combined completion xp (level 1 to 24), not a per-hour rate; low requirements but some combat involved.

Hideout) / Steal from fruit stalls (Hosidius) / Steal from the eastern bakery stall (East Ardougne market) / Quest xp block (The Queen of Thieves, The Giant Dwarf, Death to the Dorgeshuun, The Golem, Creature of Fenkenstrain, Twilight's Promise, Ethically Acquired Antiquities, The Feud, Death on the Isle).

mapMarkers.label: H.A.M. Hideout (existing marker retained -- no more-precise {{Map}} pin found on H.A.M. Hideout/H.A.M. Member pages in the sections fetched; Transportation section confirms access via charged amulet of glory to Draynor Village then ~33 tiles east)

hints.note: H.A.M. Member pickpocket chance table (H.A.M._member.s4): level 15, xp 22.2 per success, 4s stun/1-3 damage on fail (H.A.M._member.s5). Loot confirmed: gems (uncut opal 2/102, uncut jade 2/102), full H.A.M. robe set pieces (1/102 each), plus 'easy clue scrolls' per H.A.M. Hideout's own Notable features text -- the existing detail's 'gems and clue scrolls' claim is confirmed for gems, clue-scroll rate stays '??' (not itemized in the DropsTableHead sections fetched).

3 failed pickpockets within one hideout visit gets you booted or jailed (H.A.M._member.s1); a high Agility level or a full H.A.M. robe set (~4% reduction per piece, ~28% for all 7) lowers ejection chance but does NOT improve pickpocket success rate itself.
Single hideout instance per world; hop if crowded.

methods.method: Steal artefacts for Captain Khaled (Port Piscarilius)
methods.location: Port Piscarilius, after luring the two bridge guards
methods.notes: Page's fastest xp 49-65. Table gives 150,000 at 49 and 163,000 at 55 - non-uniform across the slice, so xp_hr stays ?? per the non-interpolation rule. 500-1,000 coins per heist.

methods.notes: Only viable from 55 - the very top edge of this band (mismatch flagged in the norm-s pass); it is the low-intensity option for the NEXT bands rather than this one.

Slayer as a whole is a members skill, so every method here is members-only.

methods.location: Draynor Village
methods.notes: Spria is the page's other any-level starting master, unlocked via A Porcine of Interest, giving a second easy-task pool from Draynor Village to alternate against Turael's.

methods.location: Temple Trekking minigame (start location not stated in the fetched section)
methods.notes: Page states ~40 treks/hour fully focused nets ~7,800 Slayer xp/hr (55,000 total across all reward skills); a leisurely 30 treks/hour nets ~5,800. Each trek's XP tome grants 1,100-1,650 in one random skill out of Slayer/Agility/Firemaking/Fishing/Mining/Thieving/Woodcutting -- low-commitment filler, not a primary method.

methods.method: Quest xp lump (Natural history quiz, Recipe for Disaster dwarf subquest, A Porcine of Interest, The General's Shadow)
methods.location: Various quest/miniquest lines
methods.notes: Quest-experience table lists these four with no Slayer-level gate, totalling 5,000 lump xp (1,000+1,000+1,000+2,000) -- covers the whole 1-20 band's 4,470 xp on its own; one-time rewards, not a rate.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Mazchna tasks / Vannaka tasks / Chaeldar tasks / Quest xp lump (Royal Trouble, Wanted!, Lair of Tarn Razorlor, The Heart of Darkness).

methods.location: Canifis
methods.notes: Requires Priest in Peril plus 20 combat or 99 Slayer (page's OR-gate); 6 Slayer points per task, based in Canifis. No xp/hr stated (Task-summary section is Duradel-tier only). Continuity label -- the atom chain below runs Vannaka as primary (no quest gate) with Mazchna as the optional branch, per the pre-existing oppgran chain.

methods.location: Edgeville Dungeon
methods.notes: Requires 40 combat or 99 Slayer (OR-gate), no quest; 8 Slayer points per task, based in the Edgeville Dungeon.

methods.location: Zanaris
methods.notes: Requires Lost City plus 70 combat or 99 Slayer (OR-gate); 10 Slayer points per task with a wider pool. The page's Recommended-stats section says a ~70-combat account can start directly at Chaeldar.

methods.location: Various quest/miniquest lines
methods.notes: Quest-experience table gates these at Slayer 40/none/40/48 respectively, totalling 23,000 lump xp (5,000+5,000+5,000+8,000); one-time rewards, not a rate.

methods.location: Kalphite Cave
methods.notes: Task-summary rates cannoning the soldiers at ~75,000 xp/hr and tags the task 'Do'; the locations table names the Kalphite Cave as the kalphite-dense site with no listed drawback. Cannoning costs more than the drops return here, so budget cannonballs. Members flag grounded by the skill's own infobox (Slayer is members-only).

methods.location: Kharidian Desert
methods.notes: Row's own baseline text names desert lizards as a Kharidian Desert Slayer method, but no fetched section of this page (Access-to-masters, Locations, Task summary) mentions a desert lizard assignment at all -- kept as unverified prior knowledge, no atoms authored for it.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Duradel tasks / Bloodveld (Cannon+Venator Bow, Meiyerditch Laboratories) / Dust devils (Magic barrage, Catacombs of Kourend) / Fire giants (Water spells for Magic xp).

methods.location: Shilo Village
methods.notes: Requires the Shilo Village quest plus 100 combat and 50 Slayer, or 99 Slayer alone (OR-gate); 15 Slayer points per task; the page's lead names Duradel the best master for fastest xp. Alternate: Nieve at Tree Gnome Stronghold needs just 85 combat or 99 Slayer -- her own page confirms the combat-85 gate and that Steve only REPLACES her after Monkey Madness II (the quest is not an access requirement, correcting the held note).

methods.location: Meiyerditch Laboratories
methods.notes: Task-summary table: up to 100,000 xp/hr (Cannon+Venator Bow) or 80,000 (Cannon+Melee) cannoning mutated bloodvelds in the Meiyerditch Laboratories (or Buccaneers' Laboratory); drops cover the cannon cost in Meiyerditch specifically, while cannoning this task in the Stronghold Slayer Cave loses money.

methods.method: Dust devils (Magic barrage, Catacombs of Kourend)
methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend
methods.notes: Task-summary table: 100,000-110,000 xp/hr (Magic barrage) vs 60,000 (Venator bow) / 35,000 (Melee), assignable from Slayer 65; drops cover rune costs but the Catacombs spots run crowded. Ancient Magicks needs Desert Treasure I per the recommended-quests list; no cannon can be placed in the Catacombs per the locations table.

methods.method: Fire giants (Water spells for Magic xp)
methods.notes: Task-summary table: ~40,000 xp/hr, low-effort, assignable from Slayer 1; the page recommends Water spells (100% elemental weakness) for good Magic xp and warns cannoning this task loses money.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Hydra / Thermonuclear Smoke Devil (Magic barrage, Smoke devils task) / Nechryael (Magic barrage, Catacombs of Kourend) / Abyssal demons (Venator Bow).

methods.notes: Access-to-masters states Konar quo Maten (Mount Karuulm, 75 combat or 99 Slayer) is the only master who assigns hydras, but neither that table nor the Duradel-only Task-summary gives hydra's own Slayer level or xp/hr -- left unconfirmed rather than fabricated, matching this row's existing held-data caveat.

methods.method: Thermonuclear Smoke Devil (Magic barrage, Smoke devils task)
methods.notes: Task-summary: barraging smoke devils is the page's stated best Slayer xp rate in the game, ~150,000 xp/hr (Magic) vs 55,000 (Melee), assignable from Slayer 93 (the monster's own infobox confirms the 93 gate and that Konar/Nieve/Duradel assign it); luring them takes practice per the page's own caution.

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend
methods.notes: Task-summary: ~100,000 xp/hr (Magic barrage) vs 40,000 (Melee), assignable from Slayer 80; often crowded in the Catacombs, where no cannon can be placed.

Task-summary: 53,000 xp/hr low-effort with the Venator bow (vs 80,000 Magic barrage / 40,000 Melee), assignable from Slayer 85; page says do it for abyssal whip money and block it for pure xp since it's long and commonly assigned.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Farming allotments / Bagged plants / Supercompost/ultracompost production.

hints.note: Allotment runs are a RECURRING background loop, not a one-shot action — XREF: bg:bg-farm-allotment-setup.

methods.location: allotment patch circuit -- Falador, Catherby and Ardougne are the three no-requirement sites (Morytania/Hosidius/Farming Guild/Harmony/Prifddinas/Civitas illa Fortis unlock more)
methods.notes: Plant potato (1) / onion (5) / cabbage (7) seeds, 3 per patch, and treat with compost. The training page's own allotment section is a stub; patch list and seed ladder grounded via the s16 allotment-runs list and the Allotment patch page (3 seeds per patch, 10-minute growth cycle).

methods.location: player-owned house garden (Rimmington default; Phials un-notes plants, refill watering cans east of the well)

Costs coins per plant instead of grow time.

methods.location: compost bin (e.g. Falador/Catherby patch bins)
methods.notes: Fill a compost bin with produce for 8.5 xp per supercompost bucket, or 10 xp when volcanic ash upgrades it to ultracompost; the page names watermelons and pineapples as the easiest fillers, with pineapples bought in bulk from Trader Stan's Trading Post (charter shop, non-GE).

steps.detail: Pick a method: Tree runs (oak/willow) / Fruit tree runs (apple/banana) / Farming allotments.

methods.location: fruit tree patch circuit (Catherby, Brimhaven, Lletya, etc.)
methods.notes: Apple (27) and banana (33) fruit trees give a large single-check xp dump (1,199.5 / 1,750.5) on a 16-hour growth cycle, run alongside regular tree runs.

methods.location: allotment patch circuit (continues from the prior band)

methods.location: hardwood patches: 3 on Fossil Island (Bone Voyage), 1 in Varlamore (The Ribbiting Tale...), 1 at Anglers' Retreat (Sailing 51)
methods.notes: Mahogany (55, check 15,720, pay 25 yanillian hops) and camphor (66, check 17,840, pay 10 white berries) grow ~85 hours -- a large xp dump every few days on top of regular runs.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Tree runs (magic) / Tithe Farm / Special tree runs (celastrus/redwood) / Herb runs (torstol).

methods.location: tree patch circuit (Lumbridge, Varrock, Falador Park, Taverley, Gnome Stronghold, Farming Guild; Prifddinas via Song of the Elves)
methods.notes: Magic saplings (75) are the top regular tree (check xp 13,768.3, pay 25 coconuts); the page states tree runs are the fastest effective xp in the skill even though real-time growth is slow. Yew carries 70-75 until magic unlocks.

methods.location: Hosidius, Great Kourend (instanced minigame plot)
methods.notes: MINIGAME method. Pick up to 10,000 seeds from the table inside (Golovanova 34 / Bologano 54 / Logavano 74 -- always the highest you can plant), plant into the instanced patches, water each plant 3 times before minutes 1/2/3 or it wilts, harvest at 3 minutes, deposit fruit into sacks for xp + points (Farmer Gricoller's Rewards: farmer's outfit 400 pts, seed box 250, gricoller's can 200, auto-weed 50). 'From level 74 onwards, players can get around 90,000-100,000 experience per hour' [Farming_training s20] -- access gate is 34 Farming, the cited rate needs the 74 seed tier.

methods.location: Farming Guild special patches per s7's Setup list
methods.notes: Celastrus (85, pay 8 potato cactus, check 14,130 + bark harvest) and redwood (90, pay 6 dragonfruit, check 22,450, 2,000-coin removal) add onto the regular run; celastrus bark is called out as worth the extra harvest time.

methods.location: herb patch circuit
methods.notes: Torstol (85; plant 199.5, harvest 224.5 per herb) on the standard 80-minute loop -- the page's named example of herb runs paying back the cost of tree runs.

methods.location: Port Khazard (Fishing Trawler), Zeah (Wintertodt), Lunar Isle (Vale Totems)
methods.notes: Side xp earned while training other skills, not a dedicated Construction session: Fishing Trawler grants 5 xp per fixed leak (~11,220 xp average across 44 games while earning the angler's outfit); Wintertodt grants 4x current Construction level per brazier repair; Vale Totems grants xp equal to current level per totem. No xp/hour figure given for any of the three, so ??. s11 also lists a fourth passive: from level 30, long/curved bones (rare big-bone-monster drops) hand in to Barlak in Dorgesh-Kaan (Death to the Dorgeshuun required) for Construction xp plus coins.

Oak larder's 480,000 xp/hr claim is stated only 'from level 50 onwards', which this band never reaches, so xp_hr stays ??.

Page gives no xp/hour figure (only per-repair xp), so ??. Slower than the plank ladder but cheap (3 planks + ~15 bronze nails per repair) and dual-skilling.

steps.detail: Fill compost bins with 30 pineapples then add 15 volcanic ash per bin to produce ultracompost. Ahead-of-time stockpile before herb runs.

hints.note: Direct method (no bin needed): use 2 volcanic ash on 1 bucket of already-made supercompost -> 1 bucket ultracompost, 2 ticks, 1.1 farming xp. Fastest per-bucket path if supercompost buckets are already on hand.

Compost-bin method: bin already containing supercompost + 25 volcanic ash (consumed regardless of bin fill level) -> collect 15 buckets of ultracompost via 15 empty buckets, 10 xp/bucket. Big Compost Bin (Farming Guild, 45 Farming) doubles every number: 30 buckets, 50 ash, 30 buckets to collect.
